title: `/commit` API returns 500 error if body is empty
body:
### Issue Description

The `/commit` REST API returns a 500 error, unless the body is non-empty:

```
$ curl --unix-socket $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/podman/podman.sock -H content-type:application/json  -X POST http://d/v4.0.0/commit?container=test 
{"cause":"EOF","message":"Decode(): EOF","response":500}
```

Adding a JSON body makes the request succeed:

```
$ curl --unix-socket $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/podman/podman.sock -H content-type:application/json  -X POST http://d/v4.0.0/commit?container=test -d {}
{"Id":"fcc42045bb0d9ce8f8f3a56a402bd5b5cc3c2b1bcc3e11ace7452fc12df49e6e"}
```

[Tested on v4.3.1. Apologies if this is already fixed in more recent versions; I couldn't easily figure out a way to install a later version.]


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Spin up a container
2. Try to turn it into an image with the `/commit` API (`curl --unix-socket $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/podman/podman.sock -H content-type:application/json  -X POST http://d/v4.0.0/commit?container=test`


### Describe the results you received

500 error

### Describe the results you expected

New image created

### podman info output

comments.body:
Hi @richvdh, 

Thank you for the report, I'm happy to say I can't reproduce this issue in the main branch of Podman. 

I couldn't see any code changes in the /commit endpoint code while having a quick look but the version you are running is really really old. [1]

Are all other endpoints working as expected? 
Are you testing with a known good container? 

```
curl -X POST --unix-socket /tmp/podman.sock 'http://d/v4.0.0/commit?container=awesome_easle'
{"Id":"b67693c3ff86ea3371906ff6705dc5e17d73644a43c4799794803852e7a71807"}
```

[1] https://github.com/containers/podman/releases/tag/v4.3.1

A reviewer has determined we need more information to understand the reported issue.  A comment on what is missing should be provided. Be certain you:

* provide an exact reproducer where possible
* verify you have provided all relevant information - minimum is `podman info`
* answer any follow up questions

If no response to the `needs-info` is provided in 30 days, this issue may be closed by our stale bot.

For more information on reporting issues on this repository, consult our [issue guide](ISSUE.md).
@richvdh mind closing if you are OK with @ninja-quokka response ?

Yes, other endpoints work fine, and yes, the container works fine otherwise.

> the version you are running is really really old.

It may be really old, but it's what you get if you follow the [installation instructions for Debian](https://podman.io/docs/installation#debian) ;)

But yes, I'm happy to close this if you can't reproduce on a more recent version.

> Yes, other endpoints work fine, and yes, the container works fine otherwise.
> 
> > the version you are running is really really old.
> 
> It may be really old, but it's what you get if you follow the [installation instructions for Debian](https://podman.io/docs/installation#debian) ;)
> 
> But yes, I'm happy to close this if you can't reproduce on a more recent version.

haha! Well, I guess it's nice and stable? If your adventurous, you can follow these [installation instructions](https://podman.io/docs/installation#building-from-source) instead?  

More exciting 😛
